mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an impending labor market contraction and reduced consumer spending, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates resilient manufacturing output that offsets monetary tightening pressures, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods where INDPRO increases month-over-month during documented tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 215 and FEDFUNDS remains above 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, supporting corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns are negative when PAYEMS shows month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive quarterly contractions in real GDP confirm a technical recession, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >50% of quarters following two consecutive negative GDPC1 prints.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial output and decreases in unemployment signal synchronized supply-side recovery, boosting short-term market sentiment and generating positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models.
